question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail when road resurfacing works will commence in Bryansford village.
answer_text: I have asked my officials to ensure that Bryansford village is kept under review for possible inclusion in our 2026/27 resurfacing programme and will be progressed as resources permit.In the meantime, my Department will continue to routinely inspect and repair any defects that meet the intervention level in accordance with the current Limited Service maintenance policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the (i) salary; and (ii) travel and subsistence costs, of the Civil Service Appeal Board, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: Members of the Civil Service Appeal Board (CSAB) do not receive salary for their role.  CSAB panel members are paid a fee and may claim travel and subsistence expenses via the board secretariate.The relevant figures in each of the last three financial years are set out below:Financial YearFees PaidTravel and Subsistence2022-23£7,470.0063.902023-24£474.50Nil2024-25£1053.50£63.90

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the (i) salary; and (ii) travel and subsistence costs, of The Northern Ireland Civil Service Pension Board, in each of the last three financial years.
answer_text: Members of the Northern Ireland Civil Service Pension Board do not receive a salary for their role. The independent Chair of the Board is paid fees and may claim travel and subsistence expenses via the board secretariat. The relevant figures in each of the last three financial years are set out below:Financial YearFees Paid*Travel & Subsistence2022-2023£1,648.00£02023-2024 £1,452.50£942024-2025 £3,196.50£85* Figures actually paid each financial year, including arrears.Any Pension Board member currently employed by an organisation covered by the Civil Service pension arrangements is expected to claim any travel and subsistence expenses incurred via their employer.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for his assessment of the most recent data and trends published by NISRA, on attendance at grant-aided primary, post-primary and special schools in 2024-2025.
answer_text: Regular pupil attendance at school is an on-going challenge which has been significantly impacted by Covid-19. Since schools returned to normal operation in 2021/22, we have seen very concerning levels of absence however the situation is improving.The overall attendance rate for primary, post-primary and special schools in the 2024/25 academic year was 92.1% of the total half days an increase of 0.4 percentage points from 2023/24. Whilst it is positive to see some improvement in overall attendance, there is still work to be undertaken to address those pupils impacted by chronic and severe chronic absence. My officials are currently consulting on the draft Attendance Matters Strategy, which aims to support children and young people to attend school every day.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the cost of the January 2026 report Ernst & Young LLP Report on Mental Health Strategy Action 17: Community and Voluntary Sector Review.
answer_text: Following a formal procurement process, Ernst & Young (EY) LLP was commissioned in December 2024 to conduct an independent review of the scope, scale and capacity of the community and voluntary (C&V) sector. The aim of the review was to identify how best the C&V sector can be fully integrated in the development and delivery of mental health services, ensuring improved outcomes for the population.  The total cost of the review was £112k.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 37873/22-27, for his assessment of why the Southern Health and Social Care Trust is significantly more challenged in care worker staffing than other areas.
answer_text: Home Care Services commissioned by HSC Trusts may be provided by the Trust’s own Home Care Service and/or contracted to one or more of the independent sector providers.  Each HSC Trust provides different proportions of statutory versus independent sector domiciliary care.In 2024, the highest level of in-house domiciliary care hours (i.e. provided by Trust employees) was provided by the Southern HSC Trust.  Forty-one percent of domiciliary care hours were provided by the Trust and 59% provided via the independent sector.The table below details a breakdown of domiciliary care worker vacancies actively being recruited in each HSC Trust, for the provision of statutory services, in each quarter since 30 September 2024. HSC Domiciliary Care Worker Vacancies Actively Being Recruited, by HSC Trust HSC Trust30-Sep-2431-Dec-2431-Mar-2530-Jun-2530-Sep-25Belfast77161698Northern10511310820South Eastern80426200Southern212221233207191Western5117242817Total525409274406216Source: Information & Analysis Directorate, DoHThis information highlights that the Southern HSC Trust is consistently recruiting more domiciliary care workers compared to the other HSC Trusts, in line with the fact that they provide a higher proportion of statutory domiciliary care hours.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on any progress made towards delivering a new school for Knockevin Special School, Downpatrick
answer_text: My Department is working with the Education Authority to identify a suitable site for the major capital project for Knockevin School. An Integrated Consultancy Team was appointed in November 2025 to take forward the design and oversee delivery of the project, additionally, a steering group has been established with representatives from my Department, the Education Authority and the school to receive regular updates and discuss key decisions.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any engagement he had with the Minister for the Economy from October 2025 to date, regarding NI Reval2026, and its impact on the Northern Ireland economy,
answer_text: As set out in my statement to the Assembly of 2 February 2026, I have stopped Reval 2026 in response to the concerns raised by businesses, including those in the hospitality sector, about the implications of proceeding with the draft valuations at this time. My full statement is available at:Finance Minister Oral Statement - Reval 2026 | Department of Finance

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the value of the increase in rates he included in the 2026-2027 projections in the Proposed Draft Budget 2026-29/30; and (ii) the value of the proportion of this which was attributable to NI Reval2026.
answer_text: The draft Budget shows total regional rates income is £902 million in 2026-27, £939.3 million in 2027-28 and £978.2 million in 2028-29. This includes some £255.8 over the budget period arising from the proposed increase of 5% for domestic and 3% for non domestic regional rate in each year.It should be noted that RRI principal repayments have a first call on the Regional Rates and therefore the total regional rates figure presented in the Budget is net of these repayments of £130.5m in 2026-27, £138.5 m in 2027-28 and £147.7m in 2028-29.The NI Reval2026 is revenue neutral.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail (i) the outworkings of pausing NI Reval2026 for local councils, as they set their District rate; and (ii) whether any councils will have to revise their agreed position following this pause.
answer_text: My decision to stop Reval 2026 means that councils will set their 2026/27 district rates on the basis of the current valuation list.One Council, that had formally set district rate poundage, has already taken steps to revise this.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) the current condition of the Shore Road, Rostrevor, where temporary traffic management has been reinstated; and (ii) how long the repairs will take.
answer_text: Due to the recent sustained adverse weather conditions and in light of forecasted further heavy rainfall, temporary lights were installed at the site of the 2023 landslip on Thursday 29 January 2026 as a precautionary safety measure.An inspection of the slope will be undertaken to assess the stability of the area, the outcome of which will determine if the temporary traffic management arrangements need to remain in place.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, pursuant to AQW 36133/22-27, for an update on the current level of unpaid rates.
answer_text: At the end of January 2026, rates carried forward from previous years (billed prior to 2025/26) totalled £110,120,110. This represents a £70.8 million (39.1%) reduction from the position at the end of March 2025.Of the £110.1 million;	a.	£6.5 million is in agreed payment arrangements;	b.	£54.5 million has been through a court process; and	c.	£49.1 million, not yet through a court process, continues to be pursued for payment and will, where appropriate, be considered for legal action through the Magistrates’ Courts.Unpaid rates at the end of March will continue to be pursued from April onwards.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for an update on any progress made in the management of hybrid working within the Northern Ireland Civil Service.
answer_text: The revised Civil Service Hybrid Working Policy took effect on 1 September 2025 and was fully implemented by 31 October 2025. Under the policy, staff on hybrid arrangements must spend a minimum of 40% of their weekly working hours in the workplace.To support consistent implementation and oversight, a dedicated casemanagement system has been developed and implemented. It captures standardised management information across departments, including the number of staff on hybrid working, days spent in the office, and office location data.My department issues a twiceyearly Civil Service workforce report, expanded to include metrics on hybrid Work Style Agreements. As the updated policy took effect on 31 October 2025, the first set of these metrics will be reported at the end of the 2025/26 financial year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the current number of Northern Ireland Civil Service staff on temporary contracts, compared to the same point in each of the last three years.
answer_text: Published statistics on the number of current staff employed by the Civil Service on temporary contracts, compared to the same point in each of the last three years, are available in the notes section of Table 1 in the Employment in the NICS – 1 April statistics for 2025 and 2024. For 1 April 2023, the equivalent information is available in Table 1c of the statistical report.  All reports are on the NISRA website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any AI tools used within his Department in the delivery of public services.
answer_text: I refer the Member to the answer provided to previous question AQW 34712/22-27.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the (i) number; and (ii) percentage, of (a) domestic; and (b) non-domestic, customers who have moved to using the new electronic rates portal.
answer_text: As at 3 February 2026, customers who have moved to using the new electronic rates portal are broken down as follows:                        •            3,812 Domestic occupancies;     •            544 Non Domestic occupancies; and       •            182 Mixed use occupancies.                     Within each category this equates to less than 1%.  However, it is early days, and these figures are in line with expectations.                 LPS is taking a coordinated approach to promoting the new Rates Online service, supported by a wider communications programme that includes a dedicated social media campaign ahead of the 2026/27 rate bills issuing to encourage customers to sign up.                            Paper bills that will issue from April 2026 have also been redesigned to support this transition, featuring a “Go Digital” QR code that directs customers to NI Direct for guidance and support materials as well as the links to register for Rates Online.                            The numbers continue to grow and, since the 13 January 2026 launch, are already providing valuable early insight into how customers are using the new system ahead of higher volume uptake.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of required actions in relation to the deterioration of Annalong harbour.
answer_text: My Department has no responsibility for the ownership, management or maintenance of Annalong Harbour, which is owned and managed by the local council.  Responsibility for addressing deterioration lies with the harbour owner.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the average waiting time to secure a care package, in each Health and Social Care Trust.

To ask the Minister of Finance, in light of the announcement of enhanced support to pubs in England, whether he will prioritise the ring-fencing of Barnett consequential funding to be directed to the hospitality sector in Northern Ireland.
answer_text: The amount of Barnett consequentials arising from the Chancellor’s announcement will not be confirmed until the Spring Statement in March. As Barnett consequentials are unhypothicated, it will be for the Executive to collectively determine how this funding is allocated in line with local priorities.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail (i) her level of engagement with insurance companies regarding the introduction of Graduated Driver Licensing; and (ii) her assessment of whether it will contribute to reduced insurance for young drivers.
answer_text: (i) I have directly engaged with insurance industry representatives to consider the potential effects of Graduated Driver Licensing (GDL). This included a meeting in June of last year with the Association of British Insurers (ABI), where GDL was discussed in the context of broader pressures on motor insurance costs. Additional engagement took place through written correspondence with the Motor Insurance Taskforce, of which the ABI is a member. That correspondence reaffirmed the issues previously explored in a meeting I attended with the Minister of Finance and the then British Government’s Minister for the Future of Roads, Lilian Greenwood MP. Issues discussed included local concerns about the affordability of insurance. I also emphasised that my Department remains focused on actions within its remit to help reduce the underlying risk considered by insurers, including the implementation of GDL.(ii) The central aim of GDL is to improve road safety for young and novice drivers. International evidence from New Zealand, Canada and several Australian states indicates that GDL models can result in meaningful and sustained reductions in collisions among this age group. Should comparable outcomes be achieved here, this may in turn reduce insurance claims.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health, pursuant to AQW 37682-22/27, to detail any advice available for parents who go to an Emergency Department with their child in crisis, on how to know whether a paediatrician is on duty.
answer_text: The first point I must emphasise is that, in the event of an emergency, a parent or carer should take their child directly to their nearest Emergency Department or, alternatively, phone 999. It is essential that no risk is taken with a child’s life.As outlined in my previous response to AQW 3768222/27, there is no specific policy requiring paediatricians to be on duty within Emergency Departments. However, Emergency Departments across Trusts can request paediatric input when required, just as they can seek support from any other speciality.It is important to note that, as part of the Royal College of Emergency Medicine (RCEM) curriculum, all Consultants in Emergency Medicine are required to demonstrate competence in the assessment and treatment of children and young people at all stages of development.If a parent wishes to know whether paediatricians are on duty in a specific Emergency Department, they may enquire when contacting the relevant Trust’s Phone First service. These contact details are widely available across multiple platforms.However, I would again stress that Consultants in Emergency Medicine in all Emergency Departments must be competent in treating children and young people, and parents or carers should not delay potentially critical treatment in an attempt to seek a paediatrician specifically. As stated above, in an emergency a parent or carer should take their child straight to their nearest Emergency Department or phone 999 without delay.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance, following the publication of Reval26, to detail the difference in 2025-2026 rates bills when calculated using Reval26 valuation figures, compared with the bills actually issued.
answer_text: I have taken the decision to stop Reval 2026 to create space for sectors to provide the evidence that will allow a full examination of the issues raised.  Until then, rate bills will continue to be based on the 2023 Valuation List.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the disintegration of a number of coastal roads and paths in South Down.
answer_text: While there are many roads and footways that would benefit from investment, due to limited budget resources, resurfacing work is taken forward through the prioritisation of those deemed most in need for intervention.Resurfacing schemes have recently been undertaken along a 3km stretch of the A2 Newcastle Road, Kilkeel and also along a 2.5km stretch on the A2 Strangford to Kilclief Road representing a significant investment on coastal roads.  Officials will continue to undertake regular inspections of all roads and footways to identify schemes for inclusion in future programmes and to repair defects in accordance with my Department’s Limited Service policy.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the need for a review of public transport links and services, into and throughout South Down.
answer_text: My Department is currently preparing a new suite of multimodal Transport Plans which will set the framework for transport policy and investment decisions up to 2035. For South Down, the relevant plan is the Newry, Mourne and Down Local Transport Plan (NMD LTP). This Transport Plan is being developed in an integrated manner with the Council’s Local Development Plan. The NMD LTP will focus on the main settlements of Newry, Downpatrick, Newcastle and Warrenpoint, while also considering transport needs across the wider council area. In parallel, my Department is also developing the Regional Strategic Transport Plan, which supported by data and evidence will identify the transport priorities and opportunities for the strategic transport network to 2035.Both plans are currently in development; and public and stakeholder engagement will be carried out at key stages as the plans progress.

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy for her assessment of the impact of NI Reval2026 on the economy.
answer_text: I welcome the Finance Minister's decision to halt the Reval 2026 process and to engage with businesses and others on a fair and equitable way forward.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any assessment of economic outcomes completed, in advance of issuing Reval26.
answer_text: I would refer the member to my oral statement on 2 February.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether there are any plans to introduce a scheme for landowners to renew or replace stone walls and ditches, given that they are unique to certain areas and need to be maintained.
answer_text: As Minister, I have been clear that roll out of the Farming with Nature Package is one of my key priorities within the Sustainable Agriculture Programme.  The Farming with Nature Package is being developed through extensive engagement with key agriculture and environment stakeholders, including an exercise to scope and prioritise environmental actions offered under the Farming with Nature Transition Scheme.  On that basis, and subject to required business case and legislative approvals, it is planned that support will be available for farm businesses to restore stone walls from 2028.More broadly, the Farm Sustainability Payment is designed to improve the resilience of farm businesses, which, alongside market returns, will help to support farmers to invest in their businesses for the future.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the action her Department is taking to replenish and provide salt and grit piles on rural roads, in the South Down constituency.
answer_text: My Department commits significant resources to maintaining approximately 5,850 salt bins and 52,300 grit piles across the North that are provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis on public roads which do not qualify for inclusion on the gritting schedule, providing the necessary criteria are met.Placement of grit piles at locations which meet the required criteria set out in the Winter Service policy, was carried out ahead of the winter period, taking several weeks to complete. A significant replenishment exercise commenced during the recent cold spell to target those locations most in need, however during such conditions, salt bins and grit piles can be consumed within a few hours.Given the limited resources available to the Department, it can then take some time to again replenish all boxes and piles, at a time when priority must be given to treating the main road network.  Officials endeavour to replenish salt bins and grit piles as soon as possible within the available resources.Members of the public can also request refills via NI Direct Report an issue with ice or snow | nidirect

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure whether her Department plans to publish a map providing the locations of salt bins, to ensure efficiency when requesting that they are refilled.
answer_text: My Department commits significant resources to maintaining approximately 5,850 salt bins that are provided for use by the public, on a self-help basis on public roads which do not qualify for inclusion on the gritting schedule, providing the necessary criteria are met.Salt boxes can also be removed by the department if requested to do so, or if a box isn’t being used, with some being removed during the Summer months to prevent theft or vandalism.I can confirm that my Department keeps an up-to-date register of salt and grit bin locations which is updated when salt bins are added to the network through application and successful assessment or when removed by request or through non usage.  This is used to ensure my officials are aware of the location of individual salt bins to fill ahead of the winter period, assess salt levels and order replenishments when required.NI Direct’s website has a “Refill Salt Box Bin” option within their “Report an Issue with Ice or Snow” category, however, due to salt boxes not being “fixed” items of street furniture and because they are installed and removed on request, my Department has no current plans to include their locations on a publicly available map at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail (i) whether access to a care package is dependent on a person's postcode or Health and Social Care Trust area; and (ii) the number of people who are waiting to receive a care package, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: Access to a care package is not dependent on a person’s postcode or Health and Social Care Trust area.  Entitlement to a care package is based on assessed need, under the Health and Personal Social Services framework.  All individuals, regardless of where they live have the same statutory right to request an assessment. The Trusts carry out the assessment and operate under regionally agreed eligibility criteria and the Care Management Circular, 2010.   While policy and eligibility thresholds are set regionally, delivery is organised locally by Trusts.  This may result in variations by Trust area due to the available homecare capacity.My Department receive data from each of the HSC Trusts in relation to the number of individuals awaiting full and partial packages of care.  I have listed the position as of 31st December 2025. Full Package waitsHSCT NameDec-25Belfast                            189 Northern                            532 South Eastern                              72 Southern                            764 Western                            481 NI                        2,038 Partial Package waitsHSCT NameDec-25Belfast                            380 Northern                            303 South Eastern                              11 Southern                              53 Western                            333 NI                        1,080

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy whether her Department has any plans to provide grant support for the installation of (i) solar panels; and (ii) other renewables, within domestic settings.
answer_text: Financial support is not currently available for homeowners who wish to install solar panels. The Windsor Framework enables the extension to the North of the VAT relief for energy saving materials that was previously available only in Britain. This means that people here can apply zero rates of VAT to the installation of energy-saving materials such as solar panels.Feedback from the low carbon heat consultation in 2025 suggested that combining solar panels with a heat pump could reduce overall operating costs. This option is being considered as we take forward the development of the low carbon heat and energy efficiency support scheme in 2026. In the meantime, households may still find solar panels or solar with battery storage, a worthwhile investment offering long-term savings, even in the absence of grant support.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any impact Reval2026 has had on the Civil Service estate.
answer_text: As the Member will be aware, I announced on 29 January that I was stopping the Reval 2026 process. I would direct the Member to my oral statement to the Assembly:Finance Minister Oral Statement - Reval 2026 | Department of Finance

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has any plans to open a second call for the Rural Micro Capital Grants Scheme.
answer_text: My Department does not have any plans to open a second call for the 2025/26 Rural Micro Capital Grant Scheme. Officials are working towards opening the Scheme in 2026/27, subject to a business case, funding and my approval.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the amount spent by her Department on (i) temporary traffic lights; and (ii) associated temporary traffic management, in each of the last three financial years,
answer_text: The provision of temporary traffic lights and associated temporary traffic management are required by contractors working on the carriageway to establish and maintain safe systems of work. The cost of providing these is therefore included in the overall scheme/contract costs of the works and is not separately identifiable.  Consequently, my Department does not hold expenditure information for these activities.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the type of appointment his Department issue text reminders for, as detailed in the 2024-2025 Annual Performance report which stated that over 4 million text messages were sent, helping alert customers to critical information such as appointment reminders.
To ask the Minister of Finance for his assessment of the level of spend on (i) losses; and (ii) special payments, across Departments.
answer_text: My department is responsible for approving losses and special payments for all Departments above their respective delegated limits, so each case is considered on its own merits. Individual departments disclose losses and special payments in their own Annual Report and Accounts. Under Managing Public Money NI, responsibility for the regularity and propriety of expenditure, including losses and special payments, rests with the Accounting Officers of individual departments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs for his assessment of the level of rats, including the associated risks.
answer_text: DAERA does not hold information on Brown rat numbers, but they are known to have a very widespread distribution and are strongly associated with human activity.Rats carry a range of pathogens and can cause damage and spoilage to property through gnawing and tunnelling. They can also cause ecological harm through predating birds’ nests.
department: Department of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance for his assessment of the level of direct award contracts across the public sector, including the £329 million direct award contracts within the Department of Health in 2024-2025, as highlighted by the Comptroller and Auditor General in her 2025 Report on Financial Audit Findings.
answer_text: A direct award contract must go through the individual Departments' approval process in line with appropriate Accounting Officers’ guidelines outlined in the Dear Accounting Officer Letter (DAO 06/22).These direct awards are subsequently reported and scrutinised at the relevant Audit and Risk Assurance Committee within each Department. 	In the financial year 2024/2025, DoF had five Direct Award Contracts (DACs) at a total value of £20,973,952.80. 	One of the DACs has an estimated value of £20,253,000 and was required to ensure critical service continuity for the Rates Collection Agency IT Replacement contract until the new Revenues and Benefits (R&B) Delivery Partner contract goes live. The replacement system was competitively tendered and is in the implementation phase.   	I am satisfied the appropriate processes and approvals for these DACs are in place. Details of the DoF DACs can be found on the DoF website. I have no role in scrutinising direct award contracts in other Departments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance whether data is collected to identify the number of businesses which close because of the cost of rates.
answer_text: Rate liability is one of a number of costs faced by businesses. However, decisions to cease trading can be influenced by a wide range of commercial and economic factors. The Department of Finance does not collect data that identifies reasons why individual businesses may cease trading.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the average increase to non-domestic rates bills, following the 2026 revaluation exercise.
answer_text: It is too early to comment on the impact of the 2026 revaluation exercise on nondomestic rates bills. While the draft valuation list has been published, both the regional rate and the district rate poundages for 2026–27 have not yet been set.These are required to calculate rate bills and will be determined over the coming weeks as part of the normal rate-setting process.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any work that has been completed within his Department to implement gender budgeting.
answer_text: I have met with the Gender Budgeting Project which comprises of experts from the Women’s Budgeting Group and Ulster University (UU) to explore the topic.  In my department, Budget Sustainability Division has engaged with UU and the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD) and Scottish and Welsh Government counterparts on gender budgeting.  My department has also highlighted gender budgeting resources on its website. Budget Sustainability Division is considering gender budgeting as part of its strategic Budget Improvement Plan, and further work with UU is anticipated later this year.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on progress in responses to the review of Family Fund Governance Arrangements, as detailed in his Department's Annual Report and Accounts 2024-25, given the review identified insufficient governance and assurance arrangements in place.
answer_text: The Department has been working to implement a number of actions in relation to the outstanding recommendations as detailed in the Department’s Annual Report and Account 2024-25 in regards to governance and assurance arrangements. Due to resource issues, it has not been possible to fully progress all the recommendations. To be clear there is no question over the Family Fund’s own governance, rather the review sought a number of improvements to how the Department monitors and oversees the arrangement.Internal Audit are in the process of reviewing the information provided during the follow up audit from November-December 2025.  A finalised report on their findings in regard to progressing the outstanding recommendations is expected to be available in the coming weeks.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any plans to use and maximise the National Fraud Initiative, both within his Department and across the wider Civil Service, given his Department’s central role in the delivery of shared services.
answer_text: The National Fraud Initiative exercise is co-ordinated by the NI Audit Office who correspond with all bodies involved.My Department ensures that any data matches within its responsibility are investigated and appropriate action taken.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the number of Civil Service staff who work (i) full time; (ii) part time, broken down by gender.
answer_text: Published statistics on the number of Civil Service staff at 1 April 2025 who work full time and part time, broken down by gender are available in Table 4 of the latest Employment in the NICS statistics published on the NISRA website.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the number of unallocated family support cases at the end of each of the last three financial years, broken down by Health and Social Care Trust.
answer_text: TrustMarch 2023March 2024March 2025BHSCT699448285NHSCT67110108SEHSCT241590620SHSCT303142237WHSCT173281145

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for her assessment of the process in place to address long term recurrent outages in street lighting, in areas such as the Harbour Road, Kilkeel, where lights continue to go out days after they are repaired.
answer_text: I can confirm that street lighting outage repairs are generally being carried out within the established target of 5 working days from the receipt of an initial report however some repairs, such as those requiring the replacement of underground cables, may take longer.Officials have confirmed that the outages on Harbour Road were caused by disruption to the overhead NIE network during the recent period of adverse weather. NIE has been notified and will undertake the necessary repairs. The existing lights will also be replaced with modern LEDs to help prevent future faults.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure in relation to the partial road closure at Barnmeen Road, Rathfriland, to detail (i) when a plan of repair will be in place; (ii) the cost of the existing temporary traffic management, to date; and (iii) when a two-way road will be in operation again.
answer_text: I recognise the inconvenience caused by the temporary closure of one lane on the Newry Road, Rathfriland.  The requested financial information is considered commercially sensitive.  Disclosure of such data could enable others, particularly economic competitors, to identify commercial rates and costs associated with these works, which may potentially undermine the legitimate economic interests of the Department and its contractors.However, once the scheme is complete, my Department will be able to provide the overall cost of the work.I can confirm that a work instruction has been issued to the Department’s contractor, and it is hoped that work to repair the landslip will commence in mid-February as part of a larger resurfacing scheme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Justice to detail any options available to ensure the right to a fair trial, when no solicitor or barrister agree to take on a legal case and the individual is not financially eligible for legal aid.
answer_text: The right to a fair trial is one of the fundamental rights of a democratic society and, while we have one of the highest per capita spend on legal aid, there are undoubtedly people who do not meet financial eligibility criteria for legal aid but would struggle to meet the costs privately. I want to address that gap and, as part of my Enabling Access to Justice Programme, I will shortly publish discussion papers on options for reform of financial eligibility rules and merits testing for legal aid with the aim of enhancing access to justice and addressing unmet need. I am also taking steps under the programme to improve transparency and information about the system, working with the Litigant in Person Reference Group to identify areas of most need. The need to maintain the independence of the system means the Department cannot direct cases be undertaken or recommend legal representatives but those who are struggling to secure legal representation can contact the Law Society of Northern Ireland for advice. Advice organisations such as the Law Centre NI may also be able to provide assistance.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure to detail the number of staff employed within DfI Roads, broken down by grade.
answer_text: The number of staff employed within DfI Roads on 2 February 2026 is broken down in the table below:GradeNumberProfessional & Technical StaffGrade 57Grade 610PPTO52SPTO143HPTO168PTO240TG123AdministrativeGrade 31Grade 51Grade 65Grade 728Deputy Principal31Staff Officer38Executive Officer I59Executive Officer II67Administrative Officer142Administrative Assistant30IndustrialIndustrial224Total1,406

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister whether any funding remained unspent under the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls funding programme, in the 2025-2026 financial year.
answer_text: Expenditure on the Ending Violence Against Women and Girls Programme is continually managed and monitored. There are two months of delivery remaining in the 2025-2026 financial year and spend will continue during this period. Therefore, the final funding position cannot be confirmed at this time.

question_text: To ask the Minister for Infrastructure for an update on any planned works to address the sewage and wastewater issues at Annalong Harbour.
answer_text: The management of the public water and wastewater infrastructure is an operational matter for NI Water. However, I have sought the requested information from NI Water and have been advised as follows:NI Water has advised that it met with local residents on 24 November 2025, to outline proposals for a new wastewater pumping station and network upgrade near Annalong Harbour. NI Water has also advised that planning approval for the new wastewater pumping station has been granted and discussions have commenced on the purchase of land for the wastewater pumping station. Furthermore, NI Water has confirmed that although this scheme is not currently included within the Price Control 21 (PC21) period (2021-28), it is endeavouring to put the appropriate approvals in place, so that if additional funding becomes available, this scheme may be considered for inclusion in the PC21 programme.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the estimated cost savings on (i) postage; and (ii) stationery, arising from a move to an electronic rates portal.
answer_text: Any savings in postage and stationery resulting from a move to electronic billing are dependent on customer take-up and customer preferences for continuing to receive paper bills.As adoption of eBilling through the Rates Online service increases, Land & Property Services (LPS) expects a corresponding reduction in printing and postage volumes; however, paper billing will remain necessary for customers who do not register or who prefer or need paper correspondence.Subject to these dependencies, LPS estimate potential savings of up to 20% by 2030. Actual savings realised will be driven by customer behaviour over time.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail whether there are any plans to introduce a legal requirement to register land and property with the Land Registry on the death of the owner, as part of the inheritance law process.
answer_text: There are no current plans to introduce legislation to register land and property with Land Registry on the death of an owner.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail any steps people can take to get details of their ratepayer ID, and account ID, in order to set up an online rates portal account, in advance of the 2026 rate bills being issued, if they cannot find a copy of an older bill.
answer_text: Ratepayers who do not have ready access to their previous rate bill information have a number of alternative options available.To access Rates Online, ratepayers must first complete identity authentication via the NI Direct Assurance (NIDA) service. This process provides assurance of the user’s identity. Once authenticated, ratepayers can access the Rates Online platform and use the secure messaging facility to contact Land & Property Services (LPS) to request the reference information required to link their rate account.Alternatively, ratepayers may contact LPS by telephone on 0300 200 7801.In all instances, LPS applies appropriate verification checks before disclosing account information, in accordance with data protection legislation and internal controls, to ensure that personal data is only released to the correct individual.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health to detail the proportion of assaults on healthcare workers that are under investigation by the PSNI, in each of the last three years.
answer_text: I recognise and acknowledge the issues around Violence and Aggression that Healthcare staff have to deal with in the course of carrying out their duties. Any form of violence or aggression towards staff is unacceptable and my department will continue to work with Employers and Trade Unions to ensure that appropriate protections are in place for staff.In the majority of cases, it remains a matter of choice for the individual affected as to whether or not the incident is reported to the PSNI. Statistics on such reports are not therefore held by HSC employers, however further information may be available from the Department of Justice.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Finance to detail the amount spent on university tuition fees for Civil Service staff, in each of the last three years.
answer_text: The Assistance to Study Scheme (ATS) Scheme was established to assist staff in undertaking formal academic or vocational courses, outside of work hours, that are relevant to their roles and lead to recognised qualifications. This may or may not include courses that attract university tuition fees. Financial assistance is provided subject to available resources and meeting criteria set out in the policy and procedures.The amount spent on university tuition fees for Civil Service staff within the Department of Finance is detailed in the table below.	Financial Year2022/232023/242024/25£12,063£7,715£11,021

question_text: To ask the Minister for the Economy to detail the types of financial relief her Department provides to students, against university fees.
answer_text: My Department provides tuition fee loan and grant support to eligible students undertaking designated higher education courses with eligible providers. The levels of support for academic year 2025/2026 are as follows:Full-time undergraduate students (campus-based)Tuition Fee Loan: (i) up to £4,855 if studying in NI; (ii) up to £9,535 if studying in England, Scotland or Wales; or (iii) €2,500 to cover Student Contribution Charge in the south of IrelandPart-time undergraduate students and full-time or part-time distance learning undergraduate studentsTuition Fee Grant (means-tested): up to £1,230Tuition Fee Loan: up to £3,641.25 (reduced by any Tuition Fee Grant received)Postgraduate studentsPostgraduate Tuition Fee Loan: up to £6,500 		Further information on the support available, including associated eligibility criteria, is available on the Student Finance NI website at https://www.studentfinanceni.co.uk/.

answer_text: A total of 2,496 new full-time pre-school places were provided in September 2025 in Cohort One of the Pre-School Standardisation Programme, with a further 2,382 full-time places due to be provided in September 2026 in Cohort Two of the programme.
